description: The provided image displays a 'Check for updates' icon and does not contain directly readable scientific data. However, based on the comprehensive text extraction summary for the paper 'Deciphering the composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels with machine learning', the study investigates Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C steel processed via induction melting under Ar, homogenization at 1200°C for 2 h, forging to 60×60 mm billet, secondary homogenization at 1050°C for 100 min, hot rolling to 12 mm plate, air cooling, cold rolling with 75% thickness reduction, annealing at 1000°C for 15 min, water quenching, and aging at 550°C for 2 h. Characterization combining XRD, EBSD, TEM, EPMA, and SEM revealed a single-phase austenitic (γ) matrix with recrystallized equiaxed grains of average size 17 μm and coherent nanoscale κ-carbide precipitates. Machine learning models including Random Forest (classification accuracy 0.98) and Support Vector Machine (regression RMSE <1%) predicted κ-phase existence, (γ+κ) dual-phase regions at 500–600°C, and volume fraction. The experimental validation exhibited yield strength of 953 MPa, tensile strength of 1109 MPa, and elongation of 42%.
